Integrated Annual Report 2022 04
Our Play to Win Strategy
in 2022
At the half-way point of our strategy, we’re gaining on our ambition to transform
the practice of medicine through breakthrough science. We had some big wins
in 2022 giving us the power to execute the next chapter, to go deeper into the
science, accelerate our digital know-how, and rev up our manufacturing.

Integrated Annual Report 2022 | Chasing the Miracles of Science 12
A Pipeline of Possibilities
Our approach to science begins with patients. From the moment our scientists
discover a new molecule, they have one question in mind: “How will this change
people’s lives?” We ended 2022 with 84 projects in our R&D pipeline, including
promising medicines and vaccines.
Spotlight on Clinical Trials:

Unleashing
the Potential of mRNA
Messenger RNA (mRNA) is an exciting technology that can be used to develop
powerful new vaccines and therapies. To go beyond today’s thinking, we’re
connecting scientists across the globe to explore what’s possible tomorrow.
Six of our ten vaccine candidates set for clinical Beyond vaccines, our teams are developing
trials by 2025 are powered by mRNA, including therapeutic mRNA to treat cancer and

Integrated Annual Report 2022 | Chasing the Miracles of Science 16
B ringing Solutions to
Patients with Rare Diseases
Of the 7,000 known rare diseases, only 5% currently have treatments.(1)
In 2022, we launched three new therapies, providing more solutions
for some of the 350 million people who suffer from rare diseases.
Being diagnosed with a rare disease can be a Our third approval came from EU authorities
long and lonely process; few people can relate to for Nexviadyme® to treat patients with Pompe
a patient’s experience and there is little awareness disease, a rare and deadly muscle disorder.
of the illness, all of which complicates the path
to treatment. What’s next?

of the new formulation will be developed in

its cutting-edge, wearable delivery system that
adheres to the skin.

Integrated Annual Report 2022 | Chasing the Miracles of Science 22
E volving
our Production
In 2022, we broke ground on two EVolutive Facilities (EVF) in France
and Singapore to digitalize our vaccine and biological manufacturing.
What is an EVolutive Facility? What makes our EVF sites different?
It’s a fully digitalized manufacturing site for Each site will be able to produce up to four
medicine and vaccine production. These highly different vaccines at a time or dedicate all its
innovative plants are built around a central unit capacity to a single vaccine. And that switch can
that includes completely digital production be made within just 12 days. This flexibility means
modules and are more flexible and environmentally we can quickly respond and adjust to changes
responsible than traditional facilities. in demand that can arise from, for example,
unpredictable disease outbreaks.
EVFs are also designed to have a lower
environmental footprint than traditional sites,
using renewable electricity and energy recovery
to produce vaccines. We aim to run all our sites

Integrated Annual Report 2022 | Improving People’s Lives 29
E liminating
Sleeping Sickness
About 65 million people in Sub-Saharan Africa live under the constant threat of contracting
sleeping sickness. Our new single-dose medicine, currently in clinical trials, holds the promise
of finally putting an end to this devastating tropical disease.
Sleeping sickness is deadly for people living in This simplified treatment has the potential to Guy Bongongo,
who suffers from sleeping sickness, with
remote areas, where the nearest hospital may finally bring us closer to eliminating sleeping
his father, Democratic Republic of Congo
take days to reach. This is why we are developing sickness. For patients, a rapid, close-to-home
a single-dose medicine, so patients can receive diagnosis is followed by a single dose on the same
treatment without the need for continued day, with no need to go to hospital. Healthcare
medical attention. professionals can make a diagnosis with a simple
pinprick blood test, so minimal training is required.
In November 2022, along with our partner—the
non-profit Drugs for Neglected Diseases Initiative If approved, acoziborole could represent the last
(DNDi)—we announced the successful clinical trial leg in a fight we began in 2001. It complements
results of a new single-dose oral medicine for our existing medicine, fexinidazole—the first all-
sleeping sickness, acoziborole. oral drug for sleeping sickness. This treatment
has slowed the rate of infections significantly
since its launch in 2019: cases have dropped by
97% (from 26,950 in 2001 to 805 in 2021). In 2022,

Integrated Annual Report 2022 | Improving People’s Lives 32
“All In” for
Diversity, Equity,
and Inclusion
Now in its second year, our full-scale culture transformation
is gathering pace. We’re building an organization that sees
difference as a source of strength, shaped and driven by
leadership that increasingly reflects the diversity of our
employees and the communities in which they work.
In 2022, we launched our DE&I Board, a first-of-its-kind in the pharma industry.
The board is made up of a mix of external experts and our own executives and
employees. Its goal? To make sure that we deliver on the DE&I “All In” strategy
we launched in 2021.
Yul Moldauer,
The strategy aims to ensure that our people, who represent diverse cultures, Olympic gymnast, US
communities, and over 140 nationalities, feel able to be themselves in all aspects
of their life, as well as in the communities where we work.
Paris 2024: a world
of opportunity for Sanofians
As a Premium Partner of the Paris 2024 Olympic and Paralympic
Games, we’re sponsoring Team Sanofi, made up of inspirational
athletes and coaches, each of whom champions a different aspect of
Hear from founding DE&I DE&I. We’re encouraging Sanofians to get involved in this once-in-a-
Board member, Caroline Casey, lifetime experience by cheering on Team Sanofi, becoming a volunteer
award-winning social entrepreneur at the Paris 2024 Games, or participating in the Sanofi Cup, our internal
and founder of the Valuable 500 version of the Games—which will run from 2023 to 2024. We aim
on her passion for ending to keep the Paris 2024 Games’ spirit of openness, inclusion, and
disability inequality togetherness alive, well beyond 2024.

Corporate Governance
Our governance principles ensure our leadership is diverse and representative
of our company and that we are transparent in our actions.

science, pharmaceuticals, finance, and international operations. Their role Our Executive Committee
includes monitoring our operational and financial performance, overseeing comprises 9 members,
the company’s strategic direction, and monitoring risk management. 71% headed by our CEO,
